programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: The lead prosecutor challenged the credibility of Susan Wright testifying for her own defense in her murder trial. During cross-examination, Kelly Siegler accused her of intentionally killing her husband, Jeff, and then covering up the crime. This fiery cross-examination challenged the defendant's claim that she had no choice to kill her allegedly abusive husband in their bedroom that night when he supposedly came at her with a knife.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: The lead prosecutor had more probing questions for her on the stand. The defendant admitted to lying everyone after she fatally stabbed her husband, Jeff. On the stand, Susan Wright gave a reason to the lies claiming the whole ordeal was a nightmare that she didn't fully comprehend. The defendant then claimed that after burying him in a makeshift grave in the backyard she claimed to still be afraid he was alive and would come after her.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: After 23-year-old Mary Scott fails to show up for work, a friend visits her San Diego apartment: she finds Mary's dead body, who had been raped and strangled. Mary's killer goes undetected for 51 years - until DNA finally identifies him.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1989, 14-year-old Stephanie Isaacson's body is found in the Las Vegas desert. The case goes cold for decades until a nonprofit organization provides funding for the last DNA sample recovered to be used in genealogy research. Will it succeed?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1985, the body of 23-year-old Tonya McKinley is found lying on a street in Pensacola, Florida. Detectives can't find the killer. Decades later, a new forensic science technique finally reveals who murdered Tonya that fateful New Year's Eve.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987, the body of Darlene Krashoc is discovered behind a Colorado Springs restaurant. She had been savagely beaten, raped and strangled. Investigators struggle to identify this barbaric killer. 30 years later, science provides a breakthrough.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A town in Washington lives in fear after a 17-year-old girl is abducted and raped without a suspect in sight.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The 1959 rape and murder of 9-year-old Candy Rogers in Spokane, WA is solved six decades later thanks to DNA.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Police are stumped when a toddler's body is found in Escatawpa River, leading to a decades-long mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Michelle Wyatt is found strangled to death in 1980. Few leads arise until 41 years later, thanks to forensics.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The NorCal Rapist terrorizes the women of North California for years until forensic science can identify him.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The murder of Carol Sue Klaber is cold for decades until new technology can finally bring her killer to light.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987 New Mexico, the body of 38-year-old Donna Hyatt is discovered by her daughter. Two empty glasses of cola suggest she had company the night before. It will take another 36 years before investigative genetic genealogy solves this case.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2003, a man walking in the Sierra Nevada mountains finds a human skull. In nearby Mammoth Lakes, a family is reeling after a young mother mysteriously skips town. It'll take revolutionary science to piece this puzzle together - 20 years later.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The identity of Nancy Bennallack's murderer is a mystery until the advent of investigative genetic genealogy.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A Jane Doe found by the side of the I-59 is unidentified until forensic science provides answers years later.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Huntington Beach, 1968. Boys on their way to school discover the brutalized body of a young woman in an oil field. Police are unable to identify her or her killer. It will take 52 years before DNA science is able to solve this double mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1964 Hazleton, Pennsylvania, the brutalized body of 9-year-old Marise Chiverella is discovered after she disappears walking to school. Half a century later, a teen prodigy in investigative genetic genealogy helps detectives solve this crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Police work for decades with emerging forensic sciences to identify the killer of two women in Lake Tahoe.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: 40 years after the sexual assault and murder of 20-year-old Robin Brooks in Sacramento, California, determined cold case detectives collaborate with scientists using revolutionary forensic techniques to identify her killer and put him behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1992, the decapitated body of a young woman is discovered near the Arizona Canal in Phoenix. Ten months later, another body - this time a teen - is located nearby. Dedicated detectives use forensic science to catch the infamous killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy is used to resolve the 1988 murder of a mother killed in front of her toddler.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1999, a brutal serial killer who traveled by train terrorized Texas. The FBI assembled a special task force to profile the violent drifter and help locate him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1985, a Lebanese terrorist and his four accomplices hijacked Royal Jordanian Flight 405 before taking off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990, a 14-year-old girl ran away from her Texas home and disappeared; six months later, her body was found hundreds of miles away in an Illinois barn. Agents tracked down a trucker who they believed was responsible for over 50 murders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a cold afternoon in 1989, Marine Captain Shirley Russell failed to report for duty at the base in Quantico, Virginia. FBI agents noticed her husband, a former Marine, was acting strangely. There was no body, no witness and no weapon.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A series of shootings terrorized the small town in Georgia, leaving two people dead and two others wounded.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: No suspect had emerged after the rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984. The killer was a ruthless and terrifying predator, the newest member of an infamous group known as "serial killers."

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990s, a gang based in the U.S. Virgin Islands smuggled large amounts of cocaine into mainland America. Police and federal agents targeted the violent group, but its leader remained a mystery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, the FBI and the NYPD began a joint investigation into gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. However, they soon realized Dhinsa ruled his mob-like operation with brutal force, kidnapping and killing wayward employees.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Puerto Rican separatist group known as the FALN was suspected of setting off bombs in major U.S. cities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of being a doctor but she found herself an innocent victim of drug trafficking. Agents discovered a trail of drugs, hit men and murder as they attempted to solve her kidnapping.

programme.desc: Dublin man Eric Locke refused to accept it when Sonia Blount, a mother of one, ended their relationship. After resorting to begging, stalking, threats and manipulation, he created a fake social media profile, posing as a potential love interest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Lonnie David Franklin Jr. is better known by the nickname 'Grim Sleeper.' He lured vulnerable women into his car before murdering and sexually assaulting them for more than two decades.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of serial killer Geoffrey Evans and his accomplice John Shaw, who hit headlines in 1976 after the murders of two women in Ireland.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2003, Kenneth Regan and William Horncy murdered a millionaire and his young family. The killers wiped out three generations of the Chohan family, including two young children.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2022, Danville Neil was finally jailed for the brutal 1993 murders of a WWII veteran and his sister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.desc: Steven Dean Gordon and Frank Cano murdered at least four sex workers in California between 2012 and 2013.

programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Claire began dating Christopher, she was unaware of his violent record. When he moved into her home under bail conditions, she became trapped in a dangerous cycle of control and fear that ended in tragedy.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.desc: In 1999 and 2000, John Eric Armstrong cruised the streets of Detroit, Michigan looking for sex workers.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the I-95 killer, who brutally killed six men in 1994 before being executed three years later.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 19-year-old Lori Auker left her Pennsylvania home for work and never arrived, it took space-age technology, cat hairs and insects to pinpoint the image of the woman's abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael "Mick" Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fan of murder mysteries and forensic science shows uses what she learned from TV to determine who was responsible for the accident which permanently injured her 4-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lisa Manderach and her daughter Devon left their Pennsylvania home to go shopping but didn't return home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Exploring how an American hit single from 1966 -- "The Cheater" by Bob Kuban and the In-Men -- foreshadowed the demise of the group's lead singer, Walter Scott.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Chris Ochoa confessed to the 1988 rape and murder of Nancy DePriest, the case was closed. 8 years later, Achim Marino confessed to the same crime. Can DNA evidence identify the real perpetrator?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two boys are brutally attacked, investigators rely on evidence fished out of a nearby pond. It became the first case where diatom analysis was used to place a suspect at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, the body of a young woman was discovered in an Ohio river. Most of the evidence had been washed away, but the victim's 6-year-old son unwittingly told investigators all they needed to know - without saying a word. The clue was in his genes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, a high school girl vanished after attending a party. The prime suspect had two alibi witnesses for the night she disappeared. A solid alibi can often overcome circumstantial evidence, but forensic evidence is another matter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When more than 700 Oregonians contracted food poisoning from a unique strain of salmonella in 1984, investigators uncovered a secret plot to influence local elections.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead from a gunshot wound and police suspect her husband, however, forensic teams prove what really happened, and the truth is stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, the founder of American Atheists disappeared with one of her sons, her granddaughter and $500,000 in gold coins. The police investigated several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people witness a truck driving over an unconscious man and flee into a nearby bar, but when the police turn up, the truck, the man and the witnesses are gone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered among garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A coroner finds telltale clues on the badly burned body of a murder victim, sending police in pursuit of a couple of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car is found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1987 disappearance of Houston resident Tracy Jo Shine is recalled. The investigation went cold until 2000 when a "cold case squad" learned that the woman's ex-boyfriend Michael Neal had bragged about killing her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 62-year-old man opens a car restoration business and becomes seriously ill, his doctors cannot find the cause, but forensics can; identification of a highly toxic metal in the victim's blood leads to his poisoner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After 6-year-old Michelle Lee Dorr went missing, her father confessed to murdering her. But investigators soon poked holes in his story, leaving the case unsolved for 14 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the mail-order bride of a former army sergeant goes missing, investigators look into the deaths of the man's previous two wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body. Finally, investigators use a single tooth found among some charred remains to identify her.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
